i went in with multiple problems to do with my phone and my internet bill. i was very upset because i thought i had lost all my pictures which included my husband that passed away. that was my very first concern and priority. luis and the store manager (i forgot her name) went above and beyond to help solve my problem and recovered all my pictures!! they both also helped with my storage as well and explained in detail. they also helped lower my phone bill!! i'm even eligible for a new phone that was even more exciting!! internet issue was fixed easily. both went above and beyond to help me as i was very frantic. extremely kind, compassionate and happy to help taking their time with me. they made me feel like family and that really meant alot to me. i have never had one issue which is why i choose to go into the store rather then calling customer service. they both deserve a 12 star rating for all they did!! i will be back to buy a new phone within the week and i am excited!!

we had a great sales person, tay, helping us, but we spent 3.5 hours to purchase two phones. the system was very uncooperative, the manager stepped in several times and had to enlist the call center. we have an issue with our account that we’ve never been able to resolve even with the call center. tay was very patient and personable, a great asset for your company. but as a long term customer of your company there was no excuse for taking 3.5 hours just to purchase two phones. had we gone to any other company we would not have had any issues, but because i wanted to keep our account with xfinity, it was an excruciating experience for all. i hope you can resolve these issues for others because most people would not go through this ordeal.  that is why i would give tay and the manager a 10, but the process a 0.

the first few years were great, but everything changed once the ai customer service system was introduced. getting help became frustrating, and we were constantly dealing with internet issues that required frequent modem reboots. instead of getting real support, we ended up fixing temporary problems ourselves through the app.when our promotional rate expired, our bill increased significantly. we tried to find a more affordable option, but nothing came close to what we had been paying, so we made the decision that was best for our family.what disappointed me most was the cancellation call. the representative came across as judgmental about our decision to switch providers. no customer should feel criticized for making the best financial choice for their family. we may return someday, but for now, we know we made the right decision.

i went yesterday since i had gone before to ask if i can switch to you from verizon bringing my 5 lines with me to xfinity now and i was told that i was good to go with no down payment but since i still had to stay with verizon for 2 months i had to wait but i still went ahead and got the internet and one line which they had told me it was going to be free but at the end i had to pay $20 because it was an ipad which ok i let that go and now when i went yesterday to transfer my 5 i also asked about getting new phones for at least 3 lines and when i was there i was given a different amount of credit for 2 of them and asked for a breakdown and was told that i had to wait for the trade ins and when i got home and checked my bank account i saw a deduction of $556 which to me was ridiculous so i went back today to get a breakdown and the same guy said that i forgot my receipt which was a lie since i asked for 1 yesterday and at the end it was a $300 difference and was told to call corporate and see if i can get credited the difference and now i’m thinking of returning everything and going to another provider which it’s still in the window to return everything.
